steps.title: Separate the two minerals
steps.content: The passage first describes krotite, through which all or almost all light passes, and says it is classified as transparent. The next idea shifts to beryl, which allows only some light to pass through and is classified as translucent.

steps.title: Create a sentence boundary
steps.content: Choice B gives no punctuation between the krotite description and the beryl description. Choice C uses only a comma, creating a weak run-on structure. Choice D makes the allowing phrase attach to krotite instead of beginning the separate beryl sentence. A period cleanly separates the two classifications. The answer is A.
